content.claim: Micro-level evidence from Bavaria (Böheim-Galehr & Engleitner 2014) on age-10 selection accuracy shows only 4-5% of Gymnasium students outperformed all middle-school students in German and Math, and approximately half of students not selected to Gymnasium had test results matching Gymnasium students, indicating low predictive validity of age-10 selection.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Single subnational case study; generalizability to Hungary's institutional context unknown
Compares students post-selection rather than predicting selection outcomes prospectively
No direct evidence presented in notes regarding Hungarian gimnázium selection accuracy
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
